title: Daringly Cheesy American Oyster Mushrooms Quesadilla
description: A sweet, cheese-pull, adventurous American dish featuring oyster mushrooms, katsuramuki-cut, macrobiotic-friendly.

ingredients.name: Cheddar cheese
ingredients.notes: Aged to perfection for a sharp flavor

ingredients.name: Caramelized onions
ingredients.notes: Slow-cooked in butter and a hint of thyme

ingredients.name: Red bell pepper
ingredients.notes: Sweet and crunchy for added texture

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Pungent and aromatic to complement the cheese

ingredients.name: Tortilla
ingredients.notes: Soft and pliable for easy wrapping

instructions: Preheat a large skillet or griddle over medium heat.
In the skillet, cook the butter until melted and bubbly, then add the sliced onions and cook for 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
Add the sliced red bell pepper and cook for 2 minutes, until tender.
Add the sliced oyster mushrooms and cook for 3-4 minutes, until tender and fragrant.
Sprinkle the shredded Monterey Jack and Cheddar cheese over the top of the mushroom mixture.
Place one of the large tortillas in the skillet and sprinkle shredded cheese on half of the tortilla.
Add the mushroom and cheese mixture on top of the cheese, then fold the tortilla in half to enclose.
Cook for 2-3 minutes, until the tortilla is crispy and the cheese is melted, then flip and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Repeat with the remaining ingredients to make a second quesadilla.
Cut the quesadillas into wedges and serve hot, garnished with chopped cilantro.
